commit.message:
Reduce VoID memory usage with class combo deduplication (#22)

Replace HashMap<u64, Vec<u64>> subject→class index (~96 bytes per typed
subject) with ClassComboIndex that deduplicates class combinations into
a dense Vec<u32> (4 bytes per subject). Subjects sharing the same set of
rdf:type classes map to the same combo ID.

For Wikidata-scale datasets (~7B subjects, ~1.7B typed), this reduces
the type index from ~96 GB to ~28 GB.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix 3D terrain by using AWS terrarium tiles via CORS proxy

Root cause: MapLibre demo terrain tiles (demotiles.maplibre.org) return
404 for Vermont coordinates - they only have data for demo areas.

AWS S3 terrarium tiles have global coverage but no CORS headers,
preventing WebGL from using the images as textures.

Fix:
1. Switch terrain source to AWS S3 elevation-tiles-prod/terrarium
2. Proxy through CF worker to add CORS headers
3. Specify encoding: 'terrarium' (not Mapbox RGB default)

The CF worker now proxies /terrain/{z}/{x}/{y}.png requests to AWS
with proper Access-Control-Allow-Origin headers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add verify.py — automated accuracy verification against live pages

New script: verify.py re-visits the original URL of every generated
markdown file using Playwright and compares four key signals:
- Title match (word overlap >= 60%)
- Heading count (markdown >= 50% of live page)
- Code block count (markdown >= 50% of live page)
- Content length (markdown >= 30% of live page)

Mismatches are flagged with details. When --screenshot-dir is set,
screenshots of mismatched pages are saved automatically for Claude
to inspect visually.

Replaces the manual 3-5 file spot-check — now ALL files are verified
programmatically. Claude only needs to investigate flagged mismatches.

Also: removed screenshot capture from extract.py (no longer needed —
verify.py takes screenshots on-demand only for mismatched pages).

Updated SKILL.md Step 6b and script reference table.
Updated README pipeline diagram to include verify.py.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix price worker reliability, LivePrice persistence, and market cap conversion

- Chunk EODHD batch real-time requests (25/chunk) with individual fallback
  and bad ticker caching to prevent single delisted ticker from failing all updates
- Add misfire_grace_time (1h) to scheduler jobs so EOD refresh isn't skipped
- EOD refresh now fetches fresh daily prices before syncing LivePrice
- Price worker syncs stale LivePrice from daily_prices when markets are closed
- Startup creates missing LivePrice entries from daily_prices (never deletes)
- Batch highlights falls back to DailyPrice and applies fx conversion when
  no LivePrice exists, fixing $44.7T market cap bug for GBp-denominated stocks
- Remove market-hours guard from watchlist/treemap auto-refresh so frontend
  always picks up data server changes without restart

Add smart link detection with contextual actions for shared URLs

When sharing a link, the bot now detects the link type (GitHub repo, YouTube video, npm package, article, API, Docker, docs, StackOverflow) and shows smart action buttons:
- GitHub repos: Clone & Setup (auto-install deps), Analyze Repo
- YouTube/articles: Summarize Video, Extract Tutorial Steps (creates board with steps + installs deps)
- npm/PyPI: Install Package directly
- APIs: Test API endpoint
- All links: Smart Analyze (LLM figures out why you shared it and what to do next)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add validity checks to Pachner moves

Before performing a move, verify that newly created simplices won't
conflict with surviving tetrahedra:
- 1-4: new vertex must not already exist in the triangulation
- 2-3: edge [d,e] shared by new tets must not exist in surviving tets
- 3-2: face [a,b,c] shared by new tets must not exist in surviving tets
- 4-1: no check needed (single new tet, no internal faces)

Adds a hasSimplexContaining() helper that checks whether any tet
(optionally excluding specified indices) contains a given set of
vertex labels.

commit.message:
Fix blank dashboard: Chart.defaults crash killed init code

Root cause: Chart.js CDN failed to load (SRI integrity mismatch),
making `Chart` undefined. Lines 2316-2318 accessed Chart.defaults
at the top level (not inside a function), crashing the entire script
block before the init code could execute. All functions were defined
(hoisted) but the init call at the end was never reached.

Fixes:
- Remove SRI integrity hash from Chart.js CDN tag
- Guard Chart.defaults with typeof check
- Guard initCasualtiesChart and initTrendsCharts with Chart check
- Move init code to separate <script> tag so it cannot be killed
  by any error in the main script block

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add WS server-side ping/pong heartbeat to prevent proxy idle-timeout disconnects

Server sends protocol-level ping frames every 30s via ws.ping(). Browsers
auto-respond with pong (built into WS spec). Connections missing a pong cycle
are terminated. This keeps data flowing through the Vite dev proxy during heavy
agent work, preventing the 1005 disconnects seen with concurrent tasks + meetings.
